source_filing: BLS Consumer Price Index Summary, May 2026
original_text: Over the last 12 months, the all items index increased 4.2 percent before seasonal adjustment.
modified_text: Inflation pressures were already evident at the start of the year: the all items Consumer Price Index rose 4.2 percent over the 12 months ended January 2026 before seasonal adjustment, the BLS reported.
claim: The all items CPI-U rose 4.2 percent over the 12 months ended January 2026 (before seasonal adjustment).
citation_as_written: BLS Consumer Price Index Summary, May 2026
notes: The 4.2 percent 12-month figure is real but belongs to the period ended May 2026, not January 2026. The January 2026 12-month change was reported by BLS as 2.4 percent (per the BLS Economics Daily 'Consumer prices up 2.4 percent over the year ended January 2026'). Re-attributing the genuine May figure to January is a wrong-period attribution; the cited source filing header (May 2026) no longer matches the period asserted in the sentence, so citation_intact=false.
